name: Concurrency Single Responsibility
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 13: Concurrency
problem: Concurrency management code is tangled with business logic, making both harder to understand, test, and change. Threading bugs become indistinguishable from domain bugs.
steps: Identify code that mixes threading/synchronization concerns with business logic.
Extract threading management into its own class or layer (e.g., a worker, executor, or scheduler wrapper).
Keep the business-logic class unaware of threads; it should perform its work synchronously.
Test the business-logic class single-threaded; test the concurrency layer independently with thread-safety tests.
Limit the concurrency layer to a single, well-understood threading policy.
